name: bleak (Bluetooth Low Energy Python Library)

auth.notes: BLE pairing via OS; no software auth required

performance.notes: BLE hardware latency; async Python overhead minimal; notification callbacks real-time

agent_readiness.notes: Async Bluetooth Low Energy library for Python. import asyncio. from bleak import BleakScanner, BleakClient. async def scan(): devices = await BleakScanner.discover(). async def connect(address): async with BleakClient(address) as client: services = client.services; value = await client.read_gatt_char(uuid); await client.write_gatt_char(uuid, data). Notifications: await client.start_notify(uuid, callback). Platform: macOS (CoreBluetooth), Windows (WinRT), Linux (BlueZ). Cross-platform GATT client. Used for IoT sensors, fitness trackers, medical devices, BLE beacons.

gotchas: BLE connection reliability varies by hardware and OS
macOS: Bluetooth permission required (Info.plist or runtime prompt)
Connection drops common — implement reconnect logic
BLE is async-only — synchronous wrapper needed for sync code
best_when: Reading BLE sensor data (fitness, medical, environmental)
Custom BLE device communication in Python
BLE beacon scanning and proximity detection
Automating BLE device interactions for testing/research
avoid_when: Hard real-time control (BLE latency too variable)
summary: bleak is an async cross-platform Python BLE library. BleakScanner.discover() finds BLE devices. BleakClient(address) connects. client.read_gatt_char(uuid), write_gatt_char(). start_notify(uuid, callback) for continuous sensor readings. Works on macOS (CoreBluetooth), Windows (WinRT), Linux (BlueZ). Pure Python + asyncio. Used for IoT sensors, fitness trackers, medical devices. Connection reliability varies — implement reconnect logic. MIT license.
